title: Dynamic protocol switching
abstract: Systems and methods are described to enable switching between a conventional messaging protocol and a publish-subscribe messaging protocol. Two examples of such messaging protocols include conventional CoAP and CoAP Pub/Sub. For example, improvements may be made to the existing CoRE Link attributes/formats, the existing CoAP protocol, and the W3C WoT framework to minimize waste associated with excess data exchanging and excess power consumption and account for additional performance considerations by enabling dynamic switching between CoAP and CoAP Pub/Sub. In one aspect, a new procedure for switching from CoAP to CoAP Pub/Sub is described. In another aspect, a new procedure for switching from CoAP Pub/Sub to CoAP is described. Several new CoRE link attributes are introduced to enable the dynamic switching between CoAP and CoAP Pub/Sub and vice versa. A new CoAP response code is also introduced to notify resource consumers that the communication mode used to access the resource has changed. Additionally, new WoT TD metadata attributes are introduced to enable dynamic protocol switching in the W3C WoT framework.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed: 1. An apparatus comprising a processor, a memory, and communication circuitry, the apparatus being connected to a communications network via its communication circuitry, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions stored in the memory of the apparatus which, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, cause the apparatus to: in response to an event, switch the communication protocol of a first resource from a first protocol to a second protocol, wherein the second protocol is a publish-subscribe protocol, the switch comprising: sending, via the communication network to a broker apparatus on the network, a first message indicating a request to create a second resource and including attributes set to indicate a switch in communication protocol from the first protocol to the second protocol, wherein the second resource is a copy of the first resource and the second resource is accessible via the second protocol; and receiving, via the communication network from the broker apparatus on the network, a first response message acknowledging the creating. 2. The apparatus recited in claim 1, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: send, via the communication network to the broker apparatus on the network, a request to update a content of the second resource with a content of the first resource; and receive, via the communication network from the broker apparatus on the network, a message acknowledging the updating. 3. The apparatus recited in claim 2, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: send, via the communication network to the broker apparatus on the network, a second message indicating a request to update the second resource with attributes set to identify consumers of the first resource to the second resource. 4. The apparatus recited in claim 2, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: 

 send, via the communication network to consumers of the first resource, a message including attributes set to indicate a new location of the first resource, wherein the new location is the location of the second resource. 5. The apparatus recited in claim 2, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: receive, via the communication network from a consumer of the first resource on the network, a message requesting the content of the first resource; and send, via the communication network to the consumer of the first resource on the network, a message with a message response code indicating the switch from the first protocol to the second protocol and a first attribute indicating the location of the second resource. 6. An apparatus comprising a processor, a memory, and communication circuitry, the apparatus being connected to a communications network via its communication circuitry, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions stored in the memory of the apparatus which, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, cause the apparatus to: receive, via the communication network from a first apparatus on the network, a first message indicating a request to update a first resource and including attributes set to indicate a location of the first resource and content of the first resource; send, via the communication network to the first apparatus on the network, a first response message acknowledging the updating; receive, via the communication network from a second apparatus on the network, a second message indicating a request to delete the attributes of a second resource and indicating a switch in communication protocol; send, via the communication network to the second apparatus on the network, a second response message acknowledging the deleting; and send, via the communication network to a consumer apparatus on the network, a third message including the content of the first resource. 7. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the content of the first resource, when created, is a copy of the second resource. 8. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: receive, via the communication network from a consumer of the second resource on the network, a message requesting the content of the second resource; and send, via the communication network to the consumer of the resource on the network, a message indicating the switch in communication protocol and indicating the location of the first resource. 9. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the first resource is a CoAP Pub/Sub topic and the second resource is a CoAP resource. 10. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the first resource is a CoAP resource and the second resource is a CoAP Pub/Sub topic. 11. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the switch in communication protocol is from CoAP protocol to CoAP Pub/Sub protocol. 12. The apparatus recited in claim 6, wherein the switch in communication protocol is from CoAP Pub/Sub protocol to CoAP protocol. 13. An apparatus comprising a processor, a memory, and communication circuitry, the apparatus being connected to a communications network via its communication circuitry, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions stored in the memory of the apparatus which, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, cause the apparatus to: in response to an event, switch the communications protocol of a first resource from a first protocol to a second protocol, wherein a content of the first resource is a copy of a content of a second resource at a resource provider apparatus and wherein the first protocol is a publish- subscribe protocol, the switch comprising: 

 sending, via the communication network to the resource provider apparatus on the network, a first message indicating a request to delete the first resource and including attributes set to indicate a switch in communication protocol from the first protocol to the second protocol; receiving, via the communication network from the resource provider apparatus on the network, a first response message acknowledging the switch in communication protocol; and deleting the content of the first resource. 14. The apparatus recited in claim 13, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: save a location of the second resource at the resource provider apparatus. 15. The apparatus recited in claim 13, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: receive, via the communication network from a consumer of the first resource on the network, a message requesting the content of the first resource; and send, via the communication network to the consumer of the first resource on the network, a message indicating the switch from the first protocol to the second protocol and indicating a location of the second resource. 16. The apparatus recited in claim 13, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, further cause the apparatus to: send, via the communication network to the resource provider apparatus on the network, a second message indicating a request to update the second resource with attributes set to identify consumers of the first resource to the second resource.
